questions.text: The basic structure of plasma membrane is provided by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While proteins are crucial for various functions within the membrane, they are not the primary structural component. They are embedded within the phospholipid bilayer, aiding in transport and communication.
Cholesterol contributes to membrane fluidity and stability but does not provide the basic structure of the plasma membrane itself.
The cytoskeleton supports the cell's shape and aids in intracellular transport, but it does not constitute the basic structure of the plasma membrane.
Phospholipids form the fundamental structure of the plasma membrane, creating a bilayer that serves as a barrier and medium for protein embedding.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Phospholipids because they form the bilayer that is the defining structure of the plasma membrane. This bilayer is composed of two layers of phospholipids, with hydrophilic (water-attracting) heads facing outward and hydrophobic (water-repelling) tails facing inward, creating a semi-permeable barrier.

questions.text: Out of the given options, choose the one which shows the structures found only in plants.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While plant cells have a large central vacuole and chloroplast, ribosomes are present in both plant and animal cells. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
Chloroplasts are unique to plant cells, but microtubules and peroxisomes are found in both plant and animal cells, making this option incorrect.

options.text: Chloroplast, Cell Wall, Large central Vacuole
options.explanation: This option is correct as chloroplasts, cell walls, and large central vacuoles are exclusive to plant cells, distinguishing them from animal cells.
Although plant cells have chloroplasts and cell walls, mitochondria are present in both plant and animal cells, making this option incorrect.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct because the structures listed — chloroplast, cell wall, and large central vacuole — are found only in plant cells. These structures are not present in animal cells. The other options include structures such as ribosomes, microtubules, peroxisomes, and mitochondria, which are common to both plant and animal cells, making them incorrect choices.

questions.text: Presence of large central vacuole is the characteristic of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Prokaryotic cells, such as bacteria, lack membrane-bound organelles, including vacuoles.
While some protists may have vacuoles, they generally do not possess a large central vacuole like that found in plant cells.
Fungal cells do not typically contain a large central vacuole; instead, they have smaller vacuoles that serve different functions.
Mature plant cells usually feature a large central vacuole that forms from the fusion of smaller vacuoles, playing crucial roles in storage and structural support.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Plants because mature plant cells contain a large central vacuole, which is vital for various functions, including the storage of nutrients and waste products, as well as maintaining turgor pressure to support the plant structure. In contrast, Prokaryotes (Option A) are simpler cells without membrane-bound organelles, so they do not have vacuoles. Protists (Option B) may possess vacuoles, but these are not typically large central vacuoles as seen in plants. Fungi (Option C) have smaller vacuoles and do not exhibit the same large central vacuole characteristic.

questions.text: The organelle involved in detoxification of drugs and poisons in the liver cells:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is involved in detoxification of drugs and poison.
It is involved in protein synthesis.
It is involved in cell secretions.
It has digestive or hydrolytic activity.

questions.explanation: The detoxification of drugs is the function of SER while protein synthesis is the function of RER. Mechanical support, however, is the function of both RER AND SER.

questions.text: Which one of the following organelle is found in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Centrioles are involved in cell division and are present in eukaryotic cells, but they are absent in prokaryotic cells.
The endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is a membrane-bound organelle found only in eukaryotic cells, and prokaryotic cells do not have ER.
The nucleus is a membrane-bound organelle that houses the cell's genetic material in eukaryotic cells. Prokaryotic cells lack a true nucleus.
Ribosomes are non-membranous organelles responsible for protein synthesis and are found in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.

questions.explanation: Ribosomes are the correct answer because they are non-membranous organelles that synthesize proteins and are found in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells. In contrast, centrioles, the endoplasmic reticulum, and the nucleus are membrane-bound organelles that are characteristic of eukaryotic cells and are absent in prokaryotic cells.

questions.text: Select the organelle which is only present in animal cells:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Centrioles are cylindrical structures that play a role in organizing microtubules during cell division. They are found in animal cells but are absent in most higher plant cells, which use a different mechanism for cell division.
The Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum (RER) is involved in protein synthesis and modification. It is present in both animal and plant cells, making it a common organelle across these cell types.
Microtubules are components of the cytoskeleton present in both plant and animal cells. They provide structural support and are involved in cell division, but they are not exclusive to animal cells.
Ribosomes are the sites of protein synthesis found in all eukaryotic cells, including both animal and plant cells. They are essential for translating genetic information into proteins.

questions.explanation: Centrioles are the correct answer as they are organelles specific to animal cells and are crucial for organizing microtubules during cell division. Higher plant cells do not have centrioles and rely on different structures for cell division. In contrast, the Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum (RER), microtubules, and ribosomes are present in both plant and animal cells, serving functions that are necessary in both types of eukaryotic cells.

questions.text: Ribosomes present in prokaryotes are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: 80S ribosomes are found in eukaryotic cells, not prokaryotic cells.
60S refers to the larger subunit of eukaryotic ribosomes, not the complete ribosome size in prokaryotes.
50S is the larger subunit of prokaryotic ribosomes, but it is not the complete size of the ribosomes.
70S is the correct size of ribosomes in prokaryotic cells.

questions.explanation: Prokaryotic ribosomes are characterized by a size of 70S, which is formed by the combination of a 50S large subunit and a 30S small subunit. In contrast, eukaryotic ribosomes are 80S, comprising a 60S large subunit and a 40S small subunit. Therefore, the correct answer is 70S. The options 80S and 60S are incorrect as they pertain to eukaryotic cells, while 50S is only a subunit of the prokaryotic ribosome rather than its total size.

questions.text: Functionally, mesosomes can be compared with:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribosomes are responsible for protein synthesis, not respiration.
Both mesosomes and mitochondria are involved in cellular respiration and energy production.
Polysomes are clusters of ribosomes involved in translating mRNA, unrelated to respiration.
Golgi bodies are involved in modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins, not respiration.

questions.explanation: Mesosomes in prokaryotic cells are analogous to mitochondria in eukaryotic cells because both are sites of cellular respiration. Mesosomes facilitate the electron transport chain in bacteria, similar to the role of mitochondria in eukaryotic cells. Ribosomes (Option A) are involved in protein synthesis, not respiration. Polysomes (Option C) are groups of ribosomes translating mRNA, not associated with respiration. Golgi bodies (Option D) are involved in packaging and secretion processes, unrelated to respiration.

questions.text: Fibers of the extracellular matrix are attached to_ plasma membrane:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phospholipids provide the structural framework of the plasma membrane, forming a bilayer that separates the cell from its surroundings. However, they do not directly interact with the extracellular matrix fibers.
Glycolipids, which consist of a carbohydrate attached to a lipid, are involved in cell recognition and communication but do not serve as attachment points for extracellular matrix fibers.
Proteins, specifically integrins, are the correct answer. These transmembrane proteins connect the extracellular matrix to the cell's cytoskeleton, facilitating communication and structural support.

Carbohydrates are found on the exterior of the plasma membrane, attached to proteins or lipids. They play a role in cell signaling and adhesion but are not the main components for attaching extracellular matrix fibers.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Proteins. The fibers of the extracellular matrix attach to proteins known as integrins within the plasma membrane. Integrins span the membrane and connect to both extracellular matrix proteins and intracellular cytoskeletal proteins, enabling the cell to transmit signals and maintain its structure. Phospholipids form the bilayer of the membrane but are not directly involved in binding the matrix fibers. Glycolipids, with their role in cell recognition, and Carbohydrates, involved in signaling, do not serve as attachment points for the extracellular matrix.

questions.text: _ organelles are involved in the synthesis of the plant cell wall.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The ER is involved in cell to cell communication via the plasmodesmata, and, in specialized cells, it serves as a storage site for proteins. The plant ER is also equipped with enzymes and structural proteins which are involved in the process of oil body biogenesis and lipid storage.

In plant cells, the Golgi apparatus further serves as the site at which the complex polysaccharides of the cell wall are synthesized. Golgi Apparatus is the organelle that are involved in the synthesis of the plant cell wall.
Spherosomes (or Oleosomes) are single membrane-bound cell organelles that are found only in plant cells. It is also called plant lysosome.
In plant cells, peroxisomes play vital roles in metabolism, since they house many processes including fatty acid β-oxidation, glyoxylate cycles in seedlings, and photorespiration in leaves.

questions.explanation: The Golgi and plasma membranes are the two main sites, where constituents of the cell wall are synthesized.
hints: synthesis of the plant cell wall.

questions.text: Select the pair of organs, that contains a large number of mitochondria:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While the liver contains a large number of mitochondria, the stomach does not have as many as the liver. The stomach's energy requirements are not as high as those of other organs known for high mitochondrial density.
Muscle cells contain a large number of mitochondria due to their high energy requirements, but the stomach does not share this characteristic to the same extent.
Both the heart and liver have a high number of mitochondria, but the heart has an even greater density due to its constant need for energy to pump blood.
Liver cells and muscle cells indeed contain a lot of mitochondria due to their energy requirements; however, the heart has a higher concentration compared to the liver.

Both the heart and muscle tissues require a substantial amount of energy for their functions, resulting in a high number of mitochondria in their cells. The heart is particularly noted for its high mitochondrial density, essential for its continuous pumping action.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Heart and Muscle. Both the heart and muscle tissues require a significant amount of energy due to their continuous and vigorous activities. The heart, in particular, is constantly pumping blood throughout the body, necessitating a high number of mitochondria for energy production. Muscle cells also need a lot of energy for movement and thus have many mitochondria. The other options, such as the liver, although rich in mitochondria, do not match the heart's concentration. The stomach does not have as high energy demands as these organs.

questions.text: Which of the following cells does not have a nucleus?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Muscle cells have a nucleus per cell. Different types of muscle cells have different numbers of nuclei. For example: Skeletal muscle cells are long, cylindrical, and striated. They are multinucleated meaning that they have more than one nucleus.

The Nucleus of a neuron is an oval-shaped membrane-bound structure found in the soma or body of the neuron. It contains the nucleolus and chromosomes, necessary for the coded production of proteins within the cell. The nucleolus of the nucleus produces ribosomes.
White blood cells, on the other hand, are actively working to help protect the body from infections and they need to be able to make proteins to do this and therefore must keep their nuclei.

Unlike most other eukaryotic cells, mature red blood cells don't have nuclei. When they enter the bloodstream for the first time, they eject their nuclei and organelles so they can carry more hemoglobin, and thus, more oxygen. Mature red blood cells do not need to have a nucleus because they have already made all the proteins they will ever need.

questions.explanation: Unlike most other eukaryotic cells, mature red blood cells don't have nuclei. When they enter the bloodstream for the first time, they eject their nuclei and organelles so, they can carry more hemoglobin, and thus, more oxygen. Each red blood cell has a lifespan of around 100–120 days.

questions.text: The basic structural framework of all types of membranes is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phospholipids are major membrane lipids that consist of lipid bilayers. This basic cellular structure acts as a barrier to protect the cell against various environmental insults and more importantly, enables multiple cellular processes to occur in subcellular compartments.

Glycoproteins act as cell surface markers. Glycoproteins perform vital biochemical and structural functions. They enable cells to recognize another cell as familiar or foreign, which is called cell-cell recognition. They also help cells attach to and bind to other cells, which is called cell adhesion.

Lipoproteins are formed by the combination of lipids and proteins and serve as the basic structural framework of all types of membranes in the cells. Lipoproteins help maintain the lipid composition of cell membranes..
Nucleoproteins are important for the structure and function of the cell nucleus. They are responsible for the transport of genetic material within the nucleus.

questions.explanation: Lipoproteins formed by the combination of lipids and proteins serve as the basic structural framework of all types of membranes in the cells.

questions.text: In a typical compound microscope the resolution is 2 μm. Which is about _ that of naked eye.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Naked eye resolution is 576 Megapixels which is 500 times the resolution of compound microscope
Naked eye resolution is 576 Megapixels which is 500 times the resolution of compound microscope
Naked eye resolution is 576 Megapixels which is 500 times the resolution of compound microscope
Naked eye resolution is 576 Megapixels which is 500 times the resolution of compound microscope

questions.explanation: Resolution is the measurement of the number of pixelsNaked eye resolution is 576 Megapixels which is 500 times the resolution of compound microscopeThe resolution of the naked eye refers to its ability to distinguish and perceive fine details or distinguish between two closely spaced objects. The resolution of the human eye is limited by several factors, including the number of photoreceptor cells in the retina and the optics of the eye.On average, the human eye can resolve objects that are approximately 1 arcminute apart. This means that if two objects are separated by an angle of 1 arcminute (which is 1/60th of a degree), a person with normal vision would be able to perceive them as separate entities rather than a single object.

questions.text: Which one of the following combinations is NOT part of an animal cell?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribosomes and cell membrane are present in animal cell but central vacuole is not
chloroplast is not present in animal cell but ribosomes and mitochondria are

Cell wall, chloroplast, central vacuole all three are not a part of the animal cellChatGPTIn animal cells, vacuoles are membrane-bound organelles that serve various functions depending on the type of animal and the specific cell type. While vacuoles are more prominent and diverse in plant cells, animal cells also possess certain types of vacuoles with specialized roles. Here are a few examples:Contractile Vacuoles: These vacuoles are found in certain freshwater unicellular organisms, such as Paramecium, and aid in osmoregulation. They regulate the water balance by actively pumping out excess water that enters the cell through osmosis.Food Vacuoles: In protozoans and certain types of animal cells, food vacuoles are formed during the process of phagocytosis. They engulf and digest food particles, breaking them down into simpler forms that can be utilized by the cell.Central Vacuole in some specialized cells: Certain animal cells, such as those found in sponges or some coelenterates (like hydras), may possess central vacuoles. These vacuoles function in regulating hydrostatic pressure, providing support, and aiding in buoyancy control.

Ribosomes and mitochondria are present in animal cell but cell wall is notcell wall is a rigid structure found in plant cells, bacteria, fungi, and some protists. It is a tough and protective layer located outside the cell membrane. While the cell wall serves important functions in organisms that possess it, animal cells do not have a cell wall. Here are a few reasons why animal cells lack

questions.explanation: Animal cells do not have a Cell wall and Chloroplasts. They have temporary small vacuoles; unlike plant cells which have large, permanent, central vacuoles.In the other options, at least one feature is present in the animal cells instead of all the three features pertaining to animal cells. The idea is to choose the better answer.The diagram presented below shows the major organelles in an animal cell; Flexibility and Mobility: Animal cells are often required to move and change shape, which is facilitated by their flexible cell membrane. The absence of a rigid cell wall allows animal cells to have more flexibility and mobility compared to cells with a cell wall. This flexibility is essential for various cellular processes, including cell migration, muscle contraction, and the ability to form complex tissues and organs.Intercellular Communication: Animal cells rely on direct cell-to-cell communication through specialized structures like gap junctions and tight junctions. These junctions facilitate the exchange of signals, ions, and molecules between neighboring cells, allowing for coordinated responses and tissue organization. A cell wall would hinder the direct interaction between animal cells and impede the efficient communication required for their complex physiological processes.Structural Support: In the absence of a cell wall, animal cells utilize the cytoskeleton, a network of protein filaments, to maintain their structural integrity and provide support. The cytoskeleton allows animal cells to maintain their shape, organize their internal components, and carry out cellular functions. This dynamic framework provides mechanical strength while allowing flexibility and shape changes.Adaptation to Environment: Animals have evolved to inhabit diverse environments and face varying physical conditions. The absence of a rigid cell wall enables animal cells to adapt to different osmotic pressures, temperature changes, and other environmental factors. Animal cells can regulate their volume and shape in response to external stimuli, allowing them to survive and function effectively in their respective environments.It's important to note that while animal cells lack a cell wall, they still possess a cell membrane (plasma membrane) that separates the cell's internal environment from the external environment. The cell membrane is a selectively permeable barrier that controls the passage of substances in and out of the cell.

questions.text: Name the organelle ‘’10’’ in the figure given below.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is the double membrane lining the cell. The cell wall provides structural support, protection, and shape to the cell.
There are lines present that signify grana-containing thylakoid membranes which are exclusive to chloroplast.
Ribosomes are small in size and have a single membrane.
Endoplasmic Reticulum is continuous with the nucleus membraneThe endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is a complex membranous organelle found in eukaryotic cells.

questions.explanation: There are lines present that signify grana-containing thylakoid membranes which are exclusive to chloroplast

questions.text: Which of the following offers an active barrier between the cell content and its environment by allowing only selective substances to pass through it?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cell wall is fully permeablecell wall is a rigid structure found in plant cells, bacteria, fungi, and some protists.
Plasma membrane (cell membrane) is partially permeable and allows only those substances to pass through which are required by the cell.
It is the membrane of the nucleus and allows only required substances to pass through to the nucleus.

A vacuole is a membrane-bound cell organelle. In animal cells, vacuoles are generally small and help sequester waste products. In plant cells, vacuoles help maintain water balance. Sometimes a single vacuole can take up most of the interior space of the plant cell

questions.explanation: Selectively permeable means only substances that are needed in the cell are allowed to pass throughOption A: Cell wall is fully permeableOption B: Plasma membrane (cell membrane) is partially permeable and allows only those substances to pass through which are required by the cellOption C: It is the membrane of the nucleus and allows only required substances to pass through to the nucleus Option D: A vacuole is a membrane-bound cell organelle. In animal cells, vacuoles are generally small and help sequester waste products. In plant cells, vacuoles help maintain water balance. Sometimes a single vacuole can take up most of the interior space of the plant cell

questions.text: Which among the following sentence is not correct about the organelles?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: They are found in all eukaryotic cells.
options.explanation: All eukaryotic cells contain organelles, both multicellular and unicellular.

options.text: They are only found in multicellular organisms.
options.explanation: They are found in both multicellular and unicellular.

options.text: They coordinate to produce the cell.
options.explanation: They perform cell division.

options.text: They are small sized and mostly internal.
options.explanation: Organelles have divided tasks and they're small and internal.

questions.explanation: An organelle is a subcellular structure that has one or more specific jobs to perform in the cell. Single membrane bounded organelles are found in both multicellular and unicellular organisms.

questions.text: The inner membrane of mitochondrion has many infoldings into the inner chamber, these folding are called as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cristae are the infoldings of the inner membrane of mitochondria
Cisternae refers to any of the flattened discs of the endoplasmic reticulum and golgi apparatus
Matrix is the space within the inner membrane
F1 particles contain ATP synthase which is involved in ATP synthase

questions.explanation: The infoldings increase surface area for reactions to take place Cristae are the infoldings of the inner membrane of mitochondria Cisternae refers to any of the flattened discs of the endoplasmic reticulum and golgi apparatus Matrix is the space within the inner membrane F1 particles contain ATP synthase which is involved in ATP synthase

questions.text: Select the correct matching in the following pairs.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Rough ER is primarily involved in protein synthesis due to the presence of ribosomes on its surface, not in glycogen synthesis.

options.text: Rough ER – Oxidation of fatty acids
options.explanation: Rough ER is not involved in the oxidation of fatty acids; it is primarily associated with protein synthesis.
Smooth ER is not responsible for oxidation of phospholipids; it is primarily involved in lipid synthesis and carbohydrate metabolism.
Smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) is crucial for the synthesis of lipids, including phospholipids and fatty acids, and is involved in various metabolic processes.

questions.explanation: Smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) is crucial for the synthesis of lipids, including phospholipids and fatty acids, and is involved in various metabolic processes.

questions.text: Which one of the following is not an inclusion body found in prokaryotes?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Glycogen granules serve as energy reserves and are stored in the form of inclusion bodies in prokaryotes.
Polysomes, or polyribosomes, are clusters of ribosomes translating mRNA into proteins and are not considered inclusion bodies. Therefore, they are not typical of prokaryotic inclusion bodies.
Phosphate granules are inclusion bodies that store phosphate, which is essential for energy metabolism and nucleic acid synthesis in prokaryotes.
Cyanophycean granules are storage inclusion bodies found in some prokaryotes, particularly in cyanobacteria, and are involved in storing nitrogen and carbon.

questions.explanation: Polysomes, or polyribosomes, are clusters of ribosomes translating mRNA into proteins and are not considered inclusion bodies. Therefore, they are not typical of prokaryotic inclusion bodies.

questions.text: The solid linear cytoskeletal elements having a diameter of 7 nm and made up of a single type of monomer are known as
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Microtubules are cylindrical structures made of tubulin proteins, typically about 24 nm in external diameter, though their length can vary depending on the cell type and function. They are part of the cytoskeleton and provide structural support, maintain cell shape, and facilitate intracellular transport. Microtubules also play key roles in cell division by forming the mitotic spindle.

This statement is correct. Microfilaments are thin, thread-like protein fibers about 7 nm in diameter, composed mainly of actin. They form part of the cytoskeleton, providing structural support, maintaining cell shape, and enabling cell movement. Microfilaments are also involved in processes like endocytosis, cytokinesis, and intracellular transport.

Intermediate filaments are the numerous microscopic protein fibers of about 10 nm thickness that form part of the cytoskeleton.They are intermediate in size between microtubules and microfilaments.They are made up of a variety of proteins e.g. keratin in nails.

Cilia are hair-like projections on the surface of certain eukaryotic cells, composed of microtubules arranged in a “9+2” pattern. Each microtubule has an external diameter of about 24 nm. Cilia aid in cell movement or in moving fluids and particles across the cell surface, depending on their location and function.

questions.explanation: Microtubules are hollow microscopic tubular structures with an external diameter of 24 nm and of variable length. They are composed of tubulin. Intermediate filaments are the numerous microscopic protein fibers of about 10 nm thickness that form part of the cytoskeleton. They are intermediate in size between microtubules and microfilaments.They are made up of a variety of proteins e.g. keratin in nails. Microfilaments are fine, thread-like protein fibers, 7nm in diameter. They are composed predominantly of a contractile protein called actin.

questions.text: The osmotic expansion of a cell kept in water is chiefly regulated by
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mitochondria generate ATP by cellular respiration, providing energy for the cell. They do not regulate osmotic balance, which is mainly managed by the plasma membrane and vacuoles.
Vacuoles are enclosed by a membrane and store water, nutrients, and waste. They help maintain osmotic balance and turgor pressure, supporting the cell’s structure and proper functioning.

Plastids, such as chloroplasts and amyloplasts, are responsible for photosynthesis and storing substances like starch. They do not directly regulate osmotic balance, which is mainly controlled by vacuoles and the plasma membrane.
Ribosomes synthesize proteins by translating mRNA but do not participate in water regulation or maintaining osmotic balance, which is handled by structures like vacuoles and the plasma membrane.

questions.explanation: Vacuoles are membrane-bound organelles that store nutrients and waste products, helping maintain osmotic balance and cell turgor by controlling water uptake.

questions.text: The Golgi complex plays a major role
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The Golgi apparatus modifies, sorts, and packages proteins and lipids but does not generate energy. Energy production is primarily carried out by mitochondria through cellular respiration.

options.text: In post-translational modification of proteins and glycosylation of lipids
options.explanation: The Golgi apparatus is essential for processing and modifying proteins after synthesis, including adding sugar groups to proteins and lipids. These modifications ensure proteins are functional and correctly targeted within or outside the cell.

options.text: In capturing light energy and converting it into chemical energy
options.explanation: Light energy is captured by chlorophyll in chloroplasts and converted into chemical energy through photosynthesis. The Golgi complex does not participate in energy capture or conversion; its role is in processing and packaging cellular molecules.

options.text: In the intracellular digestion of proteins and carbohydrates
options.explanation: Lysosomes carry out intracellular digestion by breaking down macromolecules, old organelles, and cellular debris using hydrolytic enzymes. The Golgi complex, in contrast, mainly modifies, sorts, and packages proteins and lipids.

questions.explanation: The Golgi complex is integral to the post-translational modification of proteins and the glycosylation of lipids. Proteins synthesized in the rough endoplasmic reticulum and lipids from the smooth endoplasmic reticulum are further processed in the Golgi apparatus. Here, proteins undergo modifications such as folding and cutting, and both proteins and lipids are glycosylated, meaning they are combined with carbohydrates to form glycoproteins and glycolipids, respectively. This is essential for their proper function and transport within the cell.

questions.text: Which one of the following organelles in the figure correctly matches with its function?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The Golgi apparatus participates in the formation of glycolipids, but it is not depicted in the figure provided. The question is about identifying the organelle visible in the figure.

The rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER), shown in the figure, is involved in protein synthesis due to the presence of ribosomes on its surface. It extends from the nuclear membrane as a network of cisternae.

options.text: Rough endoplasmic reticulum, formation of glycoproteins
options.explanation: The primary role of the rough endoplasmic reticulum is protein synthesis. While it is involved in the initial stages of glycoprotein formation, the final modification occurs in the Golgi apparatus.
The Golgi apparatus is not responsible for protein synthesis. This function is carried out by ribosomes associated with the rough endoplasmic reticulum.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: Rough endoplasmic reticulum, protein synthesis. The rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) is clearly depicted in the figure, characterized by its ribosome-studded surface, which is crucial for protein synthesis. The RER plays a significant role in synthesizing proteins intended for secretion or for use within the cell.

questions.text: A major site for synthesis of lipids is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The symplast refers to the interconnected protoplasts in plants, linked by plasmodesmata. It is involved in the transport of molecules between cells but not in lipid synthesis.
Nucleoplasm is the substance within the nucleus that suspends the organelles and is not involved in the synthesis of lipids.
The RER is associated with protein synthesis due to the presence of ribosomes on its surface, not lipid synthesis.
The Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (SER) is involved in the synthesis of lipids, including phospholipids and fatty acids. It also plays a role in the production of steroidal hormones in animal cells.

questions.explanation: The Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (SER) is the correct answer because it is specifically involved in the synthesis of lipids such as phospholipids and fatty acids. It is different from the Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum (RER), which is involved in protein synthesis due to the presence of ribosomes. The symplast is related to intercellular communication in plants, and the nucleoplasm is the fluid within the nucleus, neither of which are involved in lipid synthesis.

questions.text: The term ‘glycocalyx’ is used for:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: A layer present between cell wall and membrane of bacteria
options.explanation: This refers to the periplasmic space in gram-negative bacteria, not the glycocalyx.
The bacterial cell wall is primarily composed of peptidoglycan, which differs from the glycocalyx.

options.text: Bacterial cell engineered to possess N-glycosylated proteins
options.explanation: This pertains to genetic engineering and does not relate to the structure or function of the glycocalyx.

options.text: A layer surrounding the cell wall of bacteria
options.explanation: The glycocalyx is a sticky, gelatinous material that envelops the bacterial cell wall, forming an outer protective layer. This layer can either be a firmly attached 'capsule' or a loosely associated 'slime layer'.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D: A layer surrounding the cell wall of bacteria. The glycocalyx is a gelatinous, sticky substance that forms an additional outer layer on bacteria, serving as protection and aiding in adherence to surfaces. It can exist as a tightly bound 'capsule' or a more diffuse 'slime layer'.

questions.text: Which of the following types of plastid does not contain stored food material?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Chromoplasts are responsible for the vivid colors in plant parts, such as flowers and fruits, due to the presence of carotenoid pigments. They are not involved in food storage but rather in attracting pollinators and aiding in seed dispersal.
Elaioplasts are a type of leucoplast specialized in synthesizing and storing lipids, such as fatty acids and terpenes.
Aleuroplasts are specialized for the storage of proteins, typically found in seeds where they store protein reserves for germination.
Amyloplasts are a type of leucoplast responsible for the synthesis and storage of starch, typically found in roots and tubers.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Chromoplasts. Unlike other plastids, chromoplasts are not involved in the storage of food materials. Instead, they contain pigments that give flowers and fruits their vibrant colors, which is crucial for attracting pollinators and seed dispersers. In contrast, elaioplasts store lipids, aleuroplasts store proteins, and amyloplasts store starch. Each of these plastids plays a role in storing specific types of food materials, unlike chromoplasts.

questions.text: Select the alternative giving correct identification and function of the organelle ‘A’ in the diagram.
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Mitochondria - Produces cellular energy in the form of ATP
options.explanation: The labelled organelle is the mitochondria, known as the powerhouse of the cell, producing ATP through oxidative phosphorylation. Its inner membrane and cristae provide the surface for the electron transport chain, enabling efficient energy generation.

options.text: Golgi apparatus - Modifies, sorts, and packages proteins and lipids
options.explanation: The mitochondrion is responsible for energy production through ATP synthesis. The Golgi apparatus, in contrast, modifies, sorts, and packages proteins and lipids but does not generate energy.

options.text: Lysosomes - Contains digestive enzymes for breaking down waste
options.explanation: Lysosomes are specialized organelles that break down cellular waste, damaged organelles, and macromolecules using hydrolytic enzymes. They do not generate energy. The labelled organelle, mitochondria, is responsible for producing ATP through oxidative phosphorylation.

options.text: Endoplasmic reticulum - Synthesizes proteins and lipids
options.explanation: The endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is responsible for producing proteins and lipids needed for the cell, including membrane components and secretory proteins. It does not generate ATP. The labelled organelle, mitochondria, is the site of cellular energy production.

questions.explanation: The labelled organelle is the mitochondria, which is the site of cellular respiration and ATP production, making it the 'powerhouse' of the cell. It converts energy stored in food molecules into ATP, which is used to power various cellular processes. The other options describe organelles with different functions: the Golgi apparatus is involved in processing and packaging proteins and lipids, lysosomes contain enzymes for digestion, and the endoplasmic reticulum is involved in protein and lipid synthesis.

questions.text: Select the correct statement from the following regarding the cell membrane.
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Na+ and K+ ions move across the cell membrane by passive transport
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect because Na+ and K+ ions primarily move across the cell membrane through active transport mechanisms, specifically the sodium-potassium pump, which requires energy to function.

options.text: Proteins make up 60 to 70% of the cell membrane
options.explanation: This statement is misleading; while proteins are essential components of the cell membrane, they typically make up approximately 50 to 60% of the membrane's composition, not 60 to 70%.

options.text: Lipids are arranged in a bilayer with polar heads towards the inner part
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect because in the lipid bilayer, the polar (hydrophilic) heads of the phospholipids face outward towards the aqueous environments both inside and outside the cell, while the hydrophobic tails face inward.

options.text: Fluid mosaic model of cell membrane was proposed by Singer and Nicolson
options.explanation: This statement is correct. The fluid mosaic model, proposed by Singer and Nicolson in 1972, describes the cell membrane as a dynamic structure where a phospholipid bilayer is interspersed with various proteins, allowing for flexibility and movement.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that the fluid mosaic model of the cell membrane was proposed by Singer and Nicolson. This model illustrates the cell membrane as a flexible and dynamic structure, comprised of a lipid bilayer with proteins embedded within it, which allows for the fluidity necessary for various cellular functions. Option A is incorrect because the transport of Na+ and K+ ions primarily involves active transport, not passive transport. Option B is misleading since proteins constitute about 50 to 60% of the membrane, not 60 to 70%. Option C is also incorrect because the arrangement of lipids in a bilayer has polar heads facing outward, towards the extracellular and cytoplasmic environments, not inward.

questions.text: Which structures give a 'beads on a string' appearance when chromosomes are viewed under an electron microscope?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Genes are the smallest unit of chromosomes. Genes are fundamental units of heredity, segments of DNA on chromosomes that carry instructions for building proteins and functional RNA.
Nucleotides are building blocks of DNA, where A, T, G, and C are found with phosphate and sugar molecules.

This option is correct because a nucleosome is the most fundamental, repeating structural element of chromatin. The chromatin structure resembles beads on a thread when expanded and seen under a microscope.
Factually incorrect. There are four bases: Adenine (A), Guanine (G), Cytosine (C), and Thymine (T). These bases are found in pairs on the DNA where A pairs with T and C pairs with G.

questions.explanation: Nucleosomes appear as ‘beads ­on a ­string’ in the chromosomes when viewed under an electron microscope. The beads in ‘beads ­on a ­string’ arrangement are complexes of histones and DNA. The beads, plus the connecting DNA that leads to the next bead, form the nucleosome. A nucleosome is the fundamental unit of organisation on which the higher-order packaging of chromatin is built. The bead of each nucleosome contains eight histone molecules, in which two copies each of H2A, H2B, H3, and H4 are found.

questions.text: Having studied your paper carefully, I am convinced that neither of your answers ---- correct.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Is: This is the correct choice. The use of 'neither' indicates that we are talking about one of two options, which requires a singular verb. Therefore, 'is' correctly agrees with 'neither.'
Are: This is incorrect because 'neither' is singular and requires a singular verb form. 'Are' is plural, which does not fit the subject.
Is being: Although grammatically correct, 'is being' suggests an ongoing action that does not align with the context of finality suggested by 'having studied.' It is less appropriate than 'is.'
Are being: This option is also incorrect because, like 'are,' 'are being' fails to match the singular subject 'neither.' Thus, it is not a suitable choice.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is is because 'neither' is a singular pronoun that requires a singular verb form. In this context, the sentence indicates that out of the two provided answers, neither is correct, hence the use of 'is' is appropriate. The options 'are' and 'are being' are incorrect as they denote a plural form, which does not agree with the singular subject 'neither.' Although 'is being' is grammatically correct, it implies a continuous action that does not fit the context of the sentence, which seeks to convey a firm conclusion about the correctness of the answers.

questions.text: Choose the correct one.
questions.subject: English

options.text: And when I moved in particular direction, this time to the south and east, it shone out like a light.
options.explanation: Missing article "a" before "particular direction".

options.text: And when I moved in a particular direction, this time to south and east, it shone out like a light.
options.explanation: Missing articles "the" before "south" and "east".

options.text: And when I moved in a particular direction, this time to the south and the east, it shone out like a light.
options.explanation: The correct answer is C. And when I moved in a particular direction, this time to the south and the east, it shone out like a light. The sentence describes a specific direction, "the south and the east", which requires the use of the definite article "the" before both "south" and "east".

options.text: And when I moved in a particular direction, this time to the south and east, it shone out like a light.
options.explanation: Missing article "the" before "east".

questions.explanation: The correct answer is C. And when I moved in a particular direction, this time to the south and the east, it shone out like a light. The sentence describes a specific direction, "the south and the east", which requires the use of the definite article "the" before both "south" and "east".

questions.text: Spot the error. You really must have your own work area who can be cut off from the rest of the house.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: This word is correctly used to indicate necessity or obligation.
The error in the sentence is in option (B) "who." It should be corrected to "that" to properly refer to a work area.
This preposition is correctly used to indicate separation or distance.
This definite article is correctly used before "rest of the house."

questions.explanation: The error in the sentence is in option (B) "who." It should be corrected to "that" to properly refer to a work area.

questions.text: Find the error of punctuationThe fact remains that when the number is not simply a juxtaposition of persons;but a compact whole, then it tends to become “power".
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: This phrase correctly introduces the main clause of the sentence. There is no grammatical or punctuation error with 'The fact remains.'
This phrase is part of the dependent clause and is grammatically correct. There is no error with 'is not simply.'
While the noun 'persons' is correctly used, the punctuation before it is incorrect. A semicolon is used incorrectly where a comma should be, creating a punctuation error.
The word 'Power' is correctly capitalized within the quotation marks. There is no punctuation error related to 'Power.'

questions.explanation: The correct answer is persons because the punctuation before it is incorrect. The semicolon should be replaced with a comma to correctly separate the clauses in the sentence. 'The fact remains,' 'is not simply,' and 'Power' are all correctly used and punctuated, hence they do not contain any errors.

questions.text: Select the most suitable synonym for the given word:ALAS
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: An expression used to draw attention or express surprise, but it does not convey sadness or regret.
A common greeting or salutation, unrelated to expressing sadness or disappointment.
An expression of approval or admiration, typically used to commend someone, not to express regret or sadness.
Used to express sadness or disappointment, closely matching the tone of 'Alas'.

questions.explanation: Alas is a term used to express grief, pity or concern thus, regrettably.

questions.text: Statements: Life means stresses and strains. Stresses and strains have an adverse effect on life.Conclusions:I. Life is not worth living.II. One should avoid stresses and strains in life.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Conclusion I asserts that life is not worth living, which is a subjective assertion. The premises indicate that stresses and strains adversely affect life, but they do not imply that life itself is unworthy. Hence, this conclusion is not logically supported by the premises.

Conclusion II suggests that one should avoid stresses and strains in life. This follows logically from the premises, as they state that these factors have an adverse effect on life, implying that minimizing them would be beneficial.
This option is incorrect because Conclusion II does follow from the premises. The premises suggest that avoiding stresses and strains could improve life, thereby supporting Conclusion II.

options.text: Both conclusions I and II follow
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because while Conclusion II is logically supported by the premises, Conclusion I is not. The premises do not provide sufficient justification for the extreme assertion made in Conclusion I.

questions.explanation: The statements establish that stresses and strains are an inherent part of life and that they have a negative impact. It logically follows that avoiding these adverse effects would be beneficial, supporting Conclusion II. Conclusion I is an extreme, subjective judgment not supported by the statements.

questions.text: Find the missing number in the series: 1, 7, 3, 9, 6, 12, 10, 16, 15, ?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This is incorrect. The number 18 does not align with the identified patterns of the two interwoven series.
This is incorrect. The number 15 has already appeared in the first series and does not fit the requirements for the next number in the sequence.
This is correct. The number 21 follows the pattern of the second series, which increases by a consistent increment after 16.
This is incorrect. The number 20 does not belong to either series and fails to adhere to the established increment pattern.

questions.explanation: This is an alternating series. The first series (1, 3, 6, 10, 15) increases by +2, +3, +4, +5. The second series (7, 9, 12, 16) increases by +2, +3, +4. The next term belongs to the second series, so it is 16 + 5 = 21.

questions.text: Statements: 1. Mara runs faster than Gail. 2. Lily runs faster than Mara. 3.Gail runs faster than Lily. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: If the third statement were true, it would contradict the first two statements, which establish that Lily runs faster than both Mara and Gail.
The first two statements establish that Lily is the fastest, making it impossible for Gail to be faster than Lily. Therefore, the third statement is false.
The information given in the first two statements is sufficient to determine the truth value of the third statement. Therefore, it is not uncertain.
Since option B is the correct answer, 'none of these' cannot be the correct choice.

questions.explanation: The first two statements establish the speed order: Lily > Mara > Gail. Based on this, Lily is the fastest runner. Therefore, the statement "Gail runs faster than Lily" must be false.

questions.text: The maximum formation of mRNA occurs in the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribosomes are the organelles where protein synthesis takes place. They are located in the cytoplasm of the cell.
The nucleoplasm is the jelly-like substance that fills the nucleus of a cell. It contains the cell's DNA, as well as a variety of proteins and other molecules that are involved in gene expression.
The cytoplasm is the watery part of the cell that is located outside of the nucleus. It contains a variety of organelles, including the ribosomes.

The nucleolus is a dense region within the nucleus that is responsible for ribosome biogenesis. The mRNA is transcribed from DNA in the nucleus by an enzyme called RNA polymerase. The mRNA is then transported out of the nucleus to the cytoplasm, where it is translated into proteins by ribosomes.

questions.explanation: The maximum formation of mRNA occurs in the nucleolus. The nucleolus is a dense region within the nucleus that is responsible for ribosome biogenesis. The mRNA is transcribed from DNA in the nucleus by an enzyme called RNA polymerase. The mRNA is then transported out of the nucleus to the cytoplasm, where it is translated into proteins by ribosomes. The amount of mRNA formation varies depending on the cell type and the cell's needs. For example, cells that are rapidly dividing or that are producing a lot of protein will have higher rates of mRNA formation. The other options that you listed are not as involved in mRNA formation:Ribosomes are the organelles where protein synthesis takes place. They are located in the cytoplasm of the cell.The cytoplasm is the watery part of the cell that is located outside of the nucleus. It contains a variety of organelles, including the ribosomes.The nucleoplasm is the jelly-like substance that fills the nucleus of a cell. It contains the cell's DNA, as well as a variety of proteins and other molecules that are involved in gene expression.

questions.text: Polysome is formed by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A single ribosome is inherently made up of two major subunits (large and small), which come together to function, but this structure itself is not a polysome.

options.text: Ribosomes attached to each other in a linear arrangement
options.explanation: Ribosomes are connected via the mRNA strand they are translating, not directly attached to each other in a chain.

options.text: Several Ribosomes attached to a single mRNA
options.explanation: This option is correct because a Polysome is a cluster of ribosomes held together by a strand of messenger RNA, each of which is translating.

options.text: Many Ribosomes attached to a strand of endoplasmic reticulum
options.explanation: While many ribosomes are found attached to the rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER), they are bound there by the signal sequence on the protein they are producing, not the ER strand itself, forming the core of the polysome structure. The fundamental definition of a polysome is the complex of ribosomes and the mRNA.

questions.explanation: Ribosomes may occur in rosettes or helical groups called polyribosomes or polysomes (Gk. Poly– many, soma-body). The different ribosomes of a polyribosome are connected with a 10 -20 Å thick strand of messenger or mRNA and its maintenance requires energy. Polyribosomes are formed during periods of active protein synthesis when a number of copies of the same polypeptide are required.

questions.text: Identify the correct order of organization of genetic material from largest to smallest:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct because A genome is a complete set of DNA that includes all of its genes, non-coding DNA, mitochondrial DNA, and chloroplast DNA. It must contain all of the information required to construct and maintain that organism. It is the largest of the options presented.

This option is incorrect because chromosomes are threadlike structures composed of protein and a single molecule of DNA that is found inside the nucleus of animal and plant cells. Because the genome is the largest, its organisation comes after it.

This option is incorrect because Chromosomes are threadlike structures composed of protein and a single molecule of DNA that is found inside the nucleus of animal and plant cells. Because the genome is the largest, its organisation comes after it.

This option is incorrect because Nucleotides are nucleic acid building blocks that act as monomeric units in the nucleic acid polymers deoxyribonucleic acid and ribonucleic acid. Nucleotides follow genes.

questions.explanation: In the genome, all the genes are contained in a single set of chromosomes. The instructions in our genome are present in the form of DNA. DNA has a complicated structure in the form of a double helix. Single strands of DNA are coiled up into structures called chromosomes. Within the chromosomes, segments of DNA are “read” together to form genes. Thus, a gene is a segment of DNA or chromosome situated at a specific locus (gene locus) that carries coded information associated with a specific function and can undergo crossing over as well as mutation. A nucleotide is the basic unit of DNA made up of a pentose sugar, phosphoric acid, and a nitrogenous base.

questions.text: Percentage of DNA in a chromosome is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is incorrect as the correct one is 40%, while 60% are proteins.
Factually incorrect. The correct answer is 40%.
This option is correct because chromosomes contains 40% DNA.
Factually incorrect. The correct answer is 40%.

questions.explanation: Chromosomes are composed of DNA and histone proteins. Most are about 40 % DNA and 60 % histone proteins. A significant amount of RNA is also associated with the chromosomes.

questions.text: A portion of chromatin that is condensed only during cell division is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct because the remainder of the chromosome, called euchromatin, is condensed only during cell division.

Chromatin is a complex of DNA and proteins found in eukaryotic cells. It is the material from which the cell's chromosomes are formed and plays a crucial role in packaging, organising, and regulating gene expression in the cell.
Chromatin material which remains condensed during interphase is called heterochromatin.
Nuclear chromatin refers to the specific chromatin found within the cell nucleus.

questions.explanation: Heterochromatin and euchromatin are two types of chromatin. The key difference between heterochromatin and euchromatin is the packaging. Heterochromatin is the highly packed form of chromatin while euchromatin is the loosely packed form of chromatin and condensed only during cell division.

questions.text: Histones have an abundance of which amino acids?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Glycine is the simplest amino acid, as its side chain has only a single H-atom. Alanine is also a non-polar amino acid, but it has a hydrophobic side chain. Alanine and Glycine are not present in Histone proteins.

Tyrosine is a hydrophobic (water-hating) amino acid. Tyrosine is non-polar and has an aromatic group (conjugated planar rings) in its side chain. Cysteine is a polar amino acid, but does not have a charge. Cysteine is hydrophobic.

This option is correct because the basic amino acids that make up the histone proteins are Arginine and Lysine. Arginine is a hydrophilic (water-loving) basic amino acid. Lysine is the most basic amino acid.

Histidine is also a positively charged basic amino acid. Histidine is the amino acid with the least basic character. Serine is also a polar (dipole moment due to a charge) amino acid, but it does not have a charge. Histidine and Serine are not present in Histone proteins.

questions.explanation: Histones are rich in basic amino acids (lysine & arginine), binding tightly to negatively charged DNA.

questions.text: There are _ chromosomes in a mosquito:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct because a mosquito has 6 chromosomes in it.
This option is correct because a mosquito has 6 chromosomes in it, while a pea has 14.
This option is correct because a mosquito has 6 chromosomes in it, while drosophila has 8.
This option is correct because a mosquito has 6 chromosomes in it, while an onion has 16.

questions.explanation: The mosquito genome has 3 pairs (6 total) chromosomes.

questions.text: Chromosomes were first observed by Walther Fleming in rapidly dividing cells of the _:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Research on sea urchin embryos was conducted by Theodor Boveri, another German biologist, who used their transparent, rapidly dividing cells to study fast, repeated mitoses and contributed to the chromosome theory of inheritance, but it was after Flemming's initial observations of chromosomes.

The fruit fly (Drosophila) has also been an important model organism in genetics and chromosome biology, particularly in the 20th century for studies on gene mapping and mutations, but it was not the organism used by Flemming for the initial observation of chromosomes.
This option is correct because Walther Fleming observed rapidly dividing cells of salamanders under the microscope and observed thin thread-like structures that appeared to divide lengthwise.
Penicillium is a genus of fungus, which has a single pair of chromosomes, but it was not the organism used by Flemming for his pioneering work on mitosis and chromosomes.

questions.explanation: Walther Flemming, a German embryologist, was the first person to systematically observe and describe the behaviour of chromosomes during cell division (mitosis) in the early 1880s. He used newly developed aniline dyes to stain cells of salamander larvae embryos, which allowed him to visualise minute, thread-like structures within the nuclei.

questions.text: Histones are linked to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Factually incorrect because histones are proteins that are associated with the phosphate groups of DNA. Bases attach to a deoxyribose sugar and a phosphate group to form a nucleotide, the fundamental unit of DNA.

Histones are proteins that are associated with the phosphate groups of DNA. They form complexes with DNA by interacting with the negatively charged phosphate groups along the DNA backbone. This interaction helps to package and condense the DNA into a compact structure known as chromatin. Histones play a crucial role in DNA packaging and gene regulation within the cell nucleus. So, the correct answer is "Phosphate of DNA."
Histones are not linked to the sugar of DNA; instead, they are associated with the phosphate groups of DNA.
It is untrue as only option B is correct.

questions.explanation: Histones (positively charged proteins) bind with the negatively charged phosphate groups of DNA backbone, helping in packaging DNA into chromatin.

questions.text: Statements: I. More than two billion pounds of chemicals are spewed in the air everyday by industries and vehicles of some countries II. International pollution control organisation is expected to penalise the countries which cross the maximum emission limits.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: Statement I describes the excessive emission of pollutants, which, in turn, leads to international actions such as penalties mentioned in Statement II.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This is incorrect because the imposition of penalties does not lead to pollution; rather, it is a response to it.

options.text: Both Statement I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: While both statements address pollution, Statement II is not an independent cause but a response to the situation described in Statement I.

options.text: Both Statement I and II are effects of independent causes.
options.explanation: This is incorrect as there is a clear cause-effect relationship between the two statements, with the pollution in Statement I leading to the actions in Statement II.

questions.explanation: The massive scale of pollution (Statement I) is the problem or cause. The expected penalty from a control organization (Statement II) is the logical consequence or effect aimed at addressing this problem.

questions.text: Statement: Most of those who study in premier engineering colleges in Pakistan migrate to developed nations for better prospects. Courses of Action:I. All students joining these colleges should be asked to sign a bond to remain in Pakistan for at least ten years after they complete their education.II. All those students who desire to settle in developed nations should be asked to pay the entire cost of their education which the government subsidizes.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Wrong because forcing students to sign a 10-year bond is unrealistic, restrictive, and violates personal freedom.
Wrong because asking students to repay the entire subsidized cost is extreme and impractical as a blanket rule.
Wrong because both actions are unreasonable, so choosing either one is not justified.
Correct because both courses of action are harsh, impractical, and not fair solutions to the problem of migration.

questions.explanation: The statement highlights that many engineering graduates leave Pakistan for better opportunities. Course of action I is impractical and unethical, as it restricts personal freedom without addressing the real reasons for migration. Course of action II is also flawed, as subsidies are meant to develop national talent, not penalize choices.
